threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'corner shop' instead of 'local' and look confused when asked if you want it delivered on a bike.
Last night on the 36, a guy serenaded the whole bus with pop songs. Didn't know whether to cringe or join in, but we all laughed. Classic Peckham.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard the U-Bahn driver making announcements in German and English, then switch to Turkish for the late-night crowd. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know you're in Friedrichshain when the bus stops with a performer juggling flaming torches while you wait. Just another Tuesday here!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know not to freak out over a BART delay but still curse under their breath while juggling kids and groceries.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni stuck behind a dog at Civic Center today. Just another reminder that in SF, even our transit has unexpected guests.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real vibe is in the tiny bars and locals laughing, not just the postcard views.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they gawk at the pigeons in Plaça del Rei like they’ve never seen anything so odd before.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call the Overground the 'London Overground' instead of just the 'Gound' like everyone else here.
Heard a guy on the bus explaining why he never carries an umbrella. 'It only rains when I’m wearing my best coat.' Classic Brixton logic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a street artist painting a mural on my apartment block while kids played hopscotch in the alley—pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain could you find a bar where they serve cocktails inspired by local street art. It’s like sipping on a canvas.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they know to avoid the bus on a rainy day—traffic jams from soggy tourists are a real thing.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy casually feeding the pigeons right next to the Muni doors while everyone scrambled for a seat. Only in SF do we multitask like that.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia's plazas are just for sipping coffee, but locals know they’re vibrant hubs for spontaneous concerts and impromptu dance-offs.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to stray from La Rambla, still clutching a map like it's a lifeline.
